rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF MARY E. FERRER
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion For Reconsideration Of Order Granting Motion For Determination Of Heirship Of The Estate Of Mary E. Ferrer Aka Mary Ernestine Ferrer
rulings.Rulings: Claimant DELIA CONCEPCION FERRER'S CONCEPCION FERRER'S Motion for Reconsideration of Order Granting Motion for Determination of Heirship of the Estate of Mary E. Ferrer a.k.a.. Mary Ernestine Ferrer pursuant to CCP § 1008 is hereby tentatively denied. Claimant DELIA CONCEPCION FERRER fails to meet her burden of demonstrating the motion is based on new or different law or facts, and that she could not, with reasonable diligence, have discovered and produced the new or different matter at an earlier stage in the proceedings. Claimant filed her Petition for Letters of Administration on August, 2011, where she alleges her husband, Joseph Norberto Ferrer (Barrera) is the brother of the Decedent by virtue of both the Decedent and her husband, Joseph Norberto Ferrer, are adopted by, Ernestina Reynoso Ferrer, the paternal grandmother of Decedent. Respondent, CARLOS FERRER filed a Petition for Revocation of Letters of Administration on January 12, 2012 denying there were any adoptions. The overriding dispute in this matter has always been the same, i.e. whether the Decedent and Joseph Norberto Ferrer had been adopted by the paternal grandmother, Ernestona Reynoso Ferrer. Claimant was on notice of the dispute of heirship from the very beginning. On March 7, 2012, Barbara DeVries was issued Letters of Administration and appointed successor administrator. Successor Administrator Barbara DeVries hired Davis & Brandenburg, a genealogical firm, and on August 13, 2012, sent a letter to all beneficiaries, advising them of the genealogical search and requesting their cooperation. The Motion to Determine Heirship was filed on May 10, 2013. The Petition alleges that no formal adoption took place by paternal grandmother, Ernestina Reynoso Ferrer. The Motion to Determine Heirship set forth the reasons why Joseph Norbert Ferrer was not an heir. The hearing was set for July 31, 2013. Claimant DELIA CONCEPCION FERRER did not file a response and/or objection to DeVries' Petition to Determine Heirship. Claimant DELIA CONCEPCION FERRER fails to demonstrate why her "new" facts and/or evidence were not previously produced with reasonable diligence. She was on notice of the dispute of heirship when Respondent Carolos filed the Petition for Revocation of Letters of Administration on January 12, 2012. She was on notice that DeVries, after professional investigative agency, Brandenburger & Davis conducted more than 8 months of research, filed the Motion to Determine Heirship on May 10, 2013 and sets forth the reasons why Joseph Norbert Ferrer was not an heir. The Facts and evidence supporting the Motion for Reconsideration were all in existence even prior to Claimant filing her Petition for Administration of Letters. DELIA CONCEPCION FERRER'S Motion for Reconsideration is hereby tentatively denied. DELIA CONCEPCION FERRER'S Motion to Set Aside Order pursuant to CCP § 473 (b) is hereby tentatively denied. Claimant DELIA CONCEPCION FERRER does not provide any legal support to justify setting aside the court's order under the facts as discussed. To obtain discretionary relief under § 473(b), Claimant bears the burden to demonstrate the requisite surprise, mistake, inadvertence, or excusable neglect. Claimant fails to do so. Any party who wishes to argue the motion must notify the court (415 551-3702) and opposing counsel no later than 5:00 p.m. on November 25, 2013. If the tentative ruling is opposed, the hearing will be set on Thursday, December 5, 2013 at 2 p.m. in Department 204. If the tentative ruling is not argued, it will become the order of the court. Prevailing party to prepare the order.
